source.description: Six-panel generated marker process sheet cropped into step frames and finished art.
source.notes: Chosen for June 29 Camera Day as a bold marker/comic doodle, distinct from Sketcha.day subjects.

process_strategy: The camera body, lens, flash burst, face, buttons, strap, and marker fills appear before the final panel so the finish only reinforces existing ink and color.

final_elements.name: rounded camera body
final_elements.description: A wide rounded rectangle camera body.

final_elements.name: large circular lens
final_elements.description: A big central lens with an inner ring.

final_elements.name: comic flash burst
final_elements.description: A small starburst flash popping from the upper corner.

final_elements.name: cute face and top buttons
final_elements.description: Simple eyes, smile, and small top button shapes.

final_elements.name: strap and marker fills
final_elements.description: A side strap plus teal, yellow, pink, gray, and black marker color areas.

frames.visible_job: Draws the rounded camera body and top bump.
Adds the large lens circle and inner ring.
Adds the comic flash burst at the upper corner.
Adds eyes, smile, shutter button, and small top controls.
Adds the strap and fills the established camera, lens, face, and flash shapes with marker color.

final_step.visible_job: Only retraces existing edges, evens marker fills, and adds small highlights to already drawn shapes.
allowed_changes: small highlights on existing lens and flash

rejection_checks: No lens, flash, face, button, strap, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, ink, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
